meta.title: HR Factory — HR Consultant for Industrial SMBs
meta.description: Organizational diagnostics, HR process implementation and management coaching for SMBs of 10 to 50 employees — industrial, manufacturing, construction sectors.

hero.title: HR, forged to last.
hero.subtitle: I diagnose. I implement. I coach. So your team holds strong.

owner.title: Overwhelmed owner
owner.description: You're handling recruitment, conflicts, and evaluations on top of everything else. It works... until it doesn't.

manager.title: Manager without HR training
manager.description: Your managers are winging difficult conversations and performance reviews. Talent is walking out.

growing.title: Growing SMB
growing.description: Your ad-hoc processes can't keep up. What worked at 10 employees breaks at 30.

diagnostic.title: Diagnostic
diagnostic.description: We take stock. I analyze your current HR situation — processes, challenges, opportunities.

implementation.title: Implementation
implementation.description: We build together. I put in place lasting HR processes, adapted to your reality.

coaching.title: Coaching
coaching.description: We ensure the handoff. I train your managers to maintain these gains autonomously.

cta_final.title: Ready to structure your HR?
cta_final.subtitle: No sales pitch. Just a straight conversation about what we can do together.

quiz.title: Assess your HR maturity
quiz.subtitle: 5 quick questions to find out where you stand

q1.question: How do you handle recruitment?
q1.a: By gut feeling, when it's urgent
q1.b: We have a process, but informal

q2.question: How are performance reviews handled?
q2.b: Once a year, when we remember

q4.question: Conflict management is done...
q4.a: In firefighter mode when things blow up
q4.b: We try to resolve, but without method

q5.question: New employee onboarding...
q5.b: We have a basic first-week plan

low.title: Red zone — HR Emergency
low.description: Your HR processes are minimal. That's normal for a growing SMB, but it's costing you in turnover and time. A quick diagnostic could change everything.

mid.title: Yellow zone — Foundations to strengthen
mid.description: You have basics, but they're not solid. A few well-implemented key processes could transform your daily management.

high.title: Green zone — Optimization
high.description: Congrats! You have good HR foundations. Management coaching could bring your team to the next level.

calculator.title: How much is HR chaos costing you?
calculator.subtitle: Estimate the financial impact of employee turnover
calculator.formula: Turnover costs on average 33% of annual salary per departure

myths.title: HR Myths — What we often hear
myths.subtitle: Click to discover the reality

m1.myth: HR is only for big companies
m1.reality: Starting at 15 employees, HR issues really cost. A flawed recruitment process can cost you $50,000 per bad hire.

m2.myth: A good salary is enough to retain employees
m2.reality: Salary ranks 4th in reasons for leaving. Lack of recognition, development and bad management come first.

m3.reality: Done badly, yes. Done well, they reduce turnover by 14.9% and boost engagement. It's the most underrated tool.

m4.myth: I know my people, no need for processes
m4.reality: It works at 10 employees. At 30, it's impossible to track everything. Without processes, good intentions become blind spots.

testimonials.title: Concrete transformations
testimonials.subtitle: Real results, names changed for confidentiality

c1.before: 40% turnover. No onboarding process. Managers avoided difficult conversations.
c1.after: Turnover reduced to 18%. 90-day onboarding program. Managers trained in constructive feedback.

c2.before: Gut-feeling recruitment. 3 months to fill a position. Frequent bad hires.
c2.after: Structured process. Fill time reduced to 5 weeks. Standardized interview guide.

c3.before: No written policies. Conflicts managed case by case. Owner exhausted by HR emergencies.
c3.after: Complete employee handbook. Conflict management process. Managers autonomous for 80% of situations.

meta.title: Services — Diagnostic, Implementation, HR Coaching
meta.description: Discover my three HR support pillars for SMBs: organizational diagnostic, process implementation and management coaching.

hero.title: Services
hero.subtitle: Three pillars to structure, implement and sustain HR that holds up.

diagnostic.title: Organizational diagnostic
diagnostic.description: Understanding where you stand — no judgment.
diagnostic.item2: Audit of current HR policies, processes and practices
diagnostic.result: You know exactly what's holding you back — and how to fix it.

implementation.title: HR process implementation
implementation.description: Building processes that last — not just a document gathering dust.
implementation.result: Documented processes, your team trained to maintain them.

coaching.title: Management coaching
coaching.description: Turning your managers into confident HR leaders.
coaching.result: Autonomous managers who know how to lead their teams.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.a1: A full diagnostic ranges from $2,000 to $4,000. Process implementation from $8,000 to $15,000. I can narrow it down after our first conversation — no pricing surprises.
faq.a2: My sweet spot is SMBs with 10 to 50 employees — industrial, manufacturing, construction and services sectors. Big enough for HR issues to really cost you, not big enough for a full-time HR department.
faq.a3: Diagnostic: 2-3 weeks. Implementation: 8-12 weeks. Coaching: 3-6 months. It all depends on scope and your availability.
faq.q4: Do you offer support after the engagement?
faq.a4: Yes, optionally. Monthly check-ins, ad-hoc support, or quarterly reviews — whatever suits your needs.
faq.q5: What's the difference between a coach and an HR consultant?
faq.a5: A coach focuses on the leader's personal development. I go further: I diagnose, implement concrete processes, AND coach. It's a complete approach.
faq.q6: Do you work in French too?
faq.a6: Absolutely. I'm fully bilingual — consultations, coaching and documentation in both French and English.

meta.title: About — HR Factory
meta.description: 12 years in corporate HR leadership, 3 years as an HR consultant for industrial SMBs in Quebec. Learn about my journey and philosophy.

hero.title: About
hero.subtitle: 12 years in HR leadership. 3 years as an HR consultant for industrial SMBs.

story.title: Why I left the corporate world
story.p1: For 12 years, I was HR Director in the manufacturing sector in Quebec City. I saw firsthand: owners burning out, managers winging it, talent leaving for lack of structure.
story.p2: I was done with corporate. I wanted real impact — not just filling boxes in a system. In an SMB, when you change something, you see the difference the following week.
story.p3: I launched my practice to offer SMBs what big firms charge $50,000 a year for — but in an accessible, concrete and lasting way.

philosophy.title: My philosophy
philosophy.p1: My goal is to make you autonomous. Not to create dependency. After 6 months, your team knows how to maintain the processes. You don't need me anymore — and that's exactly what I aim for.

empathy.title: Pragmatism
empathy.description: I understand the reality of industrial SMB owners. No theory, just solutions that hold up.
empathy.example: When an owner tells me they're doing payroll at midnight, I don't judge — I help them break that cycle.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.description: You know upfront what it costs, how long it takes, and what you'll get.
transparency.example: My service proposals always include a detailed timeline, fixed budget and clear deliverables. No grey areas.

autonomy.title: Empowerment
autonomy.description: I leave when you're ready. My success is measured by your autonomy.
autonomy.example: After 6 months, your managers handle performance reviews on their own. You don't need me anymore — mission accomplished.
